about.note: The metadata provided herein has been generated using the TFL Designer Community version 2.0.2. The representation of metadata fields available in this worksheet adheres to the CDISC Analysis Results Standard example available on the ARS GitHub repository.

methods.name: Count by group for a categorical variable
methods.description: Count across groups for a categorical variable, based on subject occurrence
methods.label: Grouped count for categorical variable

codeTemplate.code:
in_data = df2_analysisidhere |>
    dplyr::select(distinctlisthere) |>
    unique() |>
    dplyr::mutate(dummy = 'dummyvar')

df3_analysisidhere <-
  cards::ard_categorical(
    data = in_data
    byhere
   ,  variables = dummy
  ) |>
dplyr::filter(stat_name == 'n') |>
 dplyr::mutate(operationid = 'opid1here')

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of all Analysis Grouping Variables, as well as the Analysis Variable (no quotes)

referencedOperationRelationships.description: The count operation whose result provides the numerator for calculation of the percentage. The referenced analysis should be the analysis that contains this percent operation.

The count operation whose result provides the denominator for calculation of the percentage. The referenced analysis should have the same analysis set and subject grouping(s) as the analysis containing this percent operation.

outputs.name: Summary of Analysis Populations

display.name: Summary of Analysis Populations
display.displayTitle: Summary of Analysis Populations

subSection.text: Summary of Analysis Populations
All Enrolled Population
Abbreviations: N=Number of subjects in the Enrolled Population; n=Number of subjects in a specific category.

Notes: Percentages were calculated as 100 × (n/N). N in column headers represents number of subjects entered in study (i.e., signed informed consent). The ITT population includes all subjects randomized. The Safety population includes all randomized subjects known to have taken at least one dose of randomized study drug. The Full Analysis Set population includes all subjects in the safety population who also have at least one post-baseline ADAS-Cog and CIBIC+assessment.
Source: adxx; Program name; Program run date/time
Analysis Population, n (%)

Note: Percentages were calculated as 100 × (n/N).

[a] A major protocol deviation is defined as any departure from the approved protocol that impacted the safety, rights, and/or welfare of the patient; negatively impacted the quality or completeness of the data; or made the informed consent document/form inaccurate.

Category, n (%)

subSection.text: Table 14.1.5.1 [PK]
Summary of [Analyte] in Plasma PK Parameters
PK Population

Abbreviations: AUC 0-∞=Area under the concentration-time curve from time zero  to infinity. AUC 0-last=Area under the concentration-time curve from time zero to the last quantifiable concentration. Cₘₐₓ=Maximum observed concentration. CL/F=Apparent oral clearance. CV%=coefficient of variation. GM=geometric mean. Max=maximum. Min=minimum. n=number of participants. PK=pharmacokinetic(s). SD=standard deviation. Tₘₐₓ=Time of maximum observed concentration. Vd/F=Apparent volume of distribution during the terminal phase.
Note: N=the number of subjects in each treatment group in the PK Population.
PK Parameter

outputs.name: Overall Survival

display.name: Overall Survival
display.displayTitle: Overall Survival

subSection.text: Table 14.2.1.1 [ONC]
Overall Survival
Efficacy Population
[a]  {Definition of “Ongoing” and “Discontinued Study” categories, and censoring rules.}
[b]  OS (months) = (date of death or censoring – date of {randomization/first dose} + 1)/30.4375, and the 95% CI is derived using log-log transformation.
[c]   Hazard ratio and 95% CIs are calculated using {the Cox proportional hazards model / statistical method}{, adjusted for randomization stratification factors (xxx) / without any adjustment}.
[d]  P value for the non-inferiority test is based on {statistical method}, with hypothesis {H0: HR >= xx vs. H1: HR < xx}.

[e]   P value for comparing the overall survival functions from the two treatment groups is from {stratified log-rank test, adjusted for randomization stratification factors (xxx) / unstratified log-rank test}.
[f] Duration of OS follow-up (months) = (date of death or censoring – date of {randomization/first dose} + 1)/30.4375.

[b]: The 95% CIs for K-M estimate were calculated using Brookmeyer Crowley method.

subSection.text: Summary of Observed and Change from Baseline by Scheduled Visits - Electrocardiogram
Safety Population 2

Abbreviations: Max=Maximum; Min=Minimum; N=Number of patients in the Safety Set; n=Number of patients in the specific category; Q1=Quartile 1 (the first quartile); Q3=Quartile 3 (the third quartile); SD=Standard Deviation.

Notes: Percentages were calculated as 100 × (n/N). Baseline is defined as the last assessment that is non-missing prior to first dose of investigational product. Change from baseline is defined as post-baseline value minus baseline value. All 12-lead ECG parameters are at the averages of triplicate readings.
Source: adxx; Program name; Program run date/time
Programming Notes: Repeat for all analysis parameters by each analysis visits as per the results terminology provided.

Parameter (Units)
   Visit
